PlatformSupportDefault.UnavailableReason: Client-only shell/personalization feature not present on Windows Server.

Entries.Name: Clear Recent Files on Logout
Entries.Description: Automatic clearing of recent files on logout
Entries.Detail: Automatically clears the recent files list each time you log out. Improves privacy on shared or multi-user machines.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Recent files are not cleared on logout
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Automatically clears the recent files list each time you log out. Improves privacy on shared or multi-user machines.

Entries.Name: Recent Files Tracking
Entries.Detail: Controls whether Windows tracks recently accessed files for display in Quick Access and Open dialogs. Disable to stop this tracking.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Recent files list tracking is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether Windows tracks recently accessed files for display in Quick Access and Open dialogs. Disable to stop this tracking.

Entries.Name: Settings Suggested Content
Entries.Detail: The Settings app can show sponsored suggestions and tips alongside your settings. Hiding removes this promotional content.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Suggested content in Settings app is shown
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The Settings app can show sponsored suggestions and tips alongside your settings. Hiding removes this promotional content.

Entries.Name: What's New in Windows
Entries.Description: Suggestions to get the most out of Windows
Entries.Detail: Shows banners and prompts highlighting new Windows features after updates. Disable to suppress these notifications.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Ways to get the most out of Windows suggestions are enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows banners and prompts highlighting new Windows features after updates. Disable to suppress these notifications.

Entries.Name: Windows Tips
Entries.Description: Tips and suggestions when using Windows
Entries.Detail: Windows shows pop-up tips and suggestions while you work. Disabling stops these interruptions.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Tips and suggestions when using Windows are enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Windows shows pop-up tips and suggestions while you work. Disabling stops these interruptions.

Entries.Name: Windows Welcome Experience
Entries.Detail: Shows a full-screen welcome page after major Windows updates highlighting new features. Disable to skip this screen.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows welcome experiences are shown
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows a full-screen welcome page after major Windows updates highlighting new features. Disable to skip this screen.

Entries.Name: All Tray Icons
Entries.Detail: Shows all notification area icons in the system tray instead of hiding overflow icons behind the expand arrow.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows hides inactive notification area icons
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows all notification area icons in the system tray instead of hiding overflow icons behind the expand arrow.

Entries.Name: Battery Percentage in System Tray
Entries.Description: Show battery percentage in the system tray (laptops only)
Entries.Detail: Sets HKCU:\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\IsBatteryPercentageEnabled. Enable writes 1; Disable deletes the value so the toggle isn't stuck off (matches the <RemoveEntry> semantics). The function short-circuits with no registry write on machines without a battery.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Battery percentage hidden by default; only the battery icon shows in the tray
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Adds the numeric battery percentage next to the tray icon so you can see remaining charge at a glance without hovering. Laptop-only — no-ops on desktops.
Entries.UnavailableReason: Requires a battery — no effect on desktops or VMs without a virtual battery

Entries.Name: Changing Sound Scheme
Entries.Detail: Controls whether users can change the Windows sound scheme. Disabling this locks the current scheme and prevents changes.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Changing sound scheme is allowed
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether users can change the Windows sound scheme. Disabling this locks the current scheme and prevents changes.

Entries.Name: Enhanced Pointer Precision
Entries.Detail: Mouse acceleration that adjusts cursor speed based on how fast you physically move the mouse. Disable for consistent raw mouse input, recommended for gaming.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Enhanced pointer precision (mouse acceleration) disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Mouse acceleration that adjusts cursor speed based on how fast you physically move the mouse. Disable for consistent raw mouse input, recommended for gaming.

Entries.Name: File Delete Confirmation
Entries.Detail: Shows a confirmation dialog before permanently deleting a file. Enable to get a prompt before files are sent to the Recycle Bin.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: No confirmation shown when deleting files
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows a confirmation dialog before permanently deleting a file. Enable to get a prompt before files are sent to the Recycle Bin.

Entries.Name: File Operations Details
Entries.Detail: Shows the detailed progress dialog for file operations (copy, move, delete) by default instead of the minimal progress bar.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: File operations shown in compact view
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows the detailed progress dialog for file operations (copy, move, delete) by default instead of the minimal progress bar.

Entries.Name: Lock Screen
Entries.Detail: Controls whether the Windows lock screen is displayed before the sign-in screen on Windows 11.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Lock screen is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether the Windows lock screen is displayed before the sign-in screen on Windows 11.

Entries.Name: Lock Screen (RS1)
Entries.Detail: Controls whether the Windows lock screen is displayed before the sign-in screen on Windows 10 (RS1 era policy path).
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Lock screen is enabled (Windows 10)
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether the Windows lock screen is displayed before the sign-in screen on Windows 10 (RS1 era policy path).

Entries.Name: Lock Screen Camera
Entries.Detail: Sets the NoLockScreenCamera Group Policy so the camera shortcut does not appear on the lock screen, removing one path to interact with the device while it is locked.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Lock-screen camera shortcut is allowed
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The lock-screen camera shortcut lets anyone with physical access take photos / videos before signing in. Blocking it shrinks the pre-auth attack surface.

Entries.Name: Block Domain PIN Logon
Entries.Description: Block convenience-PIN logon for domain accounts
Entries.Detail: Sets AllowDomainPINLogon=0 so domain accounts cannot sign in with a convenience PIN. Windows Hello for Business is unaffected since it is governed by separate policy.
Entries.CautionReason: Removes the convenience PIN sign-in option for any domain account currently using one. Users will need to fall back to password or Windows Hello for Business.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Convenience PIN logon for domain users follows GPO / Hello-for-Business state
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Convenience PIN sign-in is a separate cached credential that survives outside the Hello-for-Business model. Disabling it for domain accounts is a hardening recommendation in Microsoft and CIS baselines.

Entries.Name: Lock Screen Blur
Entries.Detail: Applies an acrylic blur effect to the sign-in screen background. Disabling shows the background without blurring.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Lock screen blur effect is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Applies an acrylic blur effect to the sign-in screen background. Disabling shows the background without blurring.

Entries.Name: Network on Lock Screen
Entries.Detail: Controls whether the network selection button is visible on the lock screen before a user has signed in.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Network selection available on lock screen
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether the network selection button is visible on the lock screen before a user has signed in.

Entries.Name: Shutdown on Lock Screen
Entries.Detail: Controls whether the shutdown and restart buttons are visible on the Windows lock screen.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Shutdown options available on lock screen
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether the shutdown and restart buttons are visible on the Windows lock screen.

Entries.Name: Startup Sound
Entries.Detail: Plays the Windows startup chime when the desktop loads after you log in. Baseline also updates the EditionOverrides compatibility value so both startup sound registry locations stay in sync.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows startup sound is disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Plays the Windows startup chime when the desktop loads after you log in. Baseline also updates the EditionOverrides compatibility value so both startup sound registry locations stay in sync.

Entries.Name: Sound Ducking Preference
Entries.Detail: Controls how Windows lowers the volume of other sounds when communications activity is detected.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows reduces other sounds by 80% during communications activity
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls how Windows lowers the volume of other sounds when communications activity is detected.

Entries.Name: Narrator Audio Ducking
Entries.Detail: Allows Narrator to lower the volume of other applications while it speaks.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Narrator lowers the volume of other apps while speaking
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Allows Narrator to lower the volume of other applications while it speaks.

Entries.Name: SpeechOneCore Voice Activation
Entries.Detail: Lets apps use the SpeechOneCore voice activation stack for wake-word and voice-command features.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: SpeechOneCore voice activation is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Lets apps use the SpeechOneCore voice activation stack for wake-word and voice-command features.

Entries.Name: Accessibility Activation Sounds
Entries.Description: Sounds when accessibility features are activated
Entries.Detail: Plays sounds when accessibility features such as Sticky Keys or Filter Keys are activated.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Accessibility activation sounds are enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Plays sounds when accessibility features such as Sticky Keys or Filter Keys are activated.

Entries.Name: Accessibility Warning Sounds
Entries.Detail: Plays warning sounds when accessibility-related features are activated or when warning conditions are triggered.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Accessibility warning sounds are enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Plays warning sounds when accessibility-related features are activated or when warning conditions are triggered.

Entries.Name: Task Manager Details
Entries.Detail: Opens Task Manager directly to the Details tab showing all processes instead of the simplified summary view.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Task Manager opens in summary view
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Opens Task Manager directly to the Details tab showing all processes instead of the simplified summary view.

Entries.Name: Title Bar Color
Entries.Detail: Colors window title bars to match your Windows accent color instead of showing them in plain white or grey.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Title bar color not adapted to background
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Colors window title bars to match your Windows accent color instead of showing them in plain white or grey.

Entries.Name: Visual Effects
Entries.Detail: Controls the overall visual effects setting. Performance disables animations for a snappier feel. Appearance enables all effects.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Visual effects set to best Appearance
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls the overall visual effects setting. Performance disables animations for a snappier feel. Appearance enables all effects.

Entries.Name: Aero Shake
Entries.Description: Shake title bar to minimize other windows
Entries.Detail: Shaking a window title bar minimizes all other open windows. Disable if you find this triggers by accident during normal use.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Shaking title bar does not minimize other windows
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shaking a window title bar minimizes all other open windows. Disable if you find this triggers by accident during normal use.

Entries.Name: App Color Mode
Entries.Detail: Sets the default color mode that applications use when they respect the system setting: Dark or Light.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: App mode set to Light
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the default color mode that applications use when they respect the system setting: Dark or Light.

Entries.Name: Build Number on Desktop
Entries.Detail: Displays the Windows build number and edition as a watermark in the bottom right corner of your desktop.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Build number not shown on desktop
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Displays the Windows build number and edition as a watermark in the bottom right corner of your desktop.

Entries.Name: Control Panel View
Entries.Detail: Sets the default display style for Control Panel to Large Icons, Small Icons, or the default Category grouping.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Control Panel icons shown by category
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the default display style for Control Panel to Large Icons, Small Icons, or the default Category grouping.

Entries.Name: Downloads Folder Grouping
Entries.Detail: Controls whether files in the Downloads folder are grouped by date modified. Setting to None shows all files in a flat list.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Downloads folder grouped by date modified
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether files in the Downloads folder are grouped by date modified. Setting to None shows all files in a flat list.

Entries.Name: Encrypted/Compressed File Color
Entries.Detail: Shows encrypted files in green and compressed files in blue in File Explorer. Makes these file types immediately identifiable.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Encrypted/compressed files shown in color
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows encrypted files in green and compressed files in blue in File Explorer. Makes these file types immediately identifiable.

Entries.Name: Explorer Title Full Path
Entries.Description: Full directory path in Explorer title bar
Entries.Detail: Shows the complete folder path in the File Explorer title bar instead of just the folder name. Useful when working with many folders of the same name.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Full path not shown in Explorer title bar
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows the complete folder path in the File Explorer title bar instead of just the folder name. Useful when working with many folders of the same name.

Entries.Name: File Explorer Compact Mode
Entries.Detail: Reduces the spacing between items in File Explorer for a denser, more information-rich list view.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: File Explorer compact mode is disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Reduces the spacing between items in File Explorer for a denser, more information-rich list view.

Entries.Name: File Extensions
Entries.Detail: Shows the file extension (e.g. .txt, .exe) after every filename in Explorer. Helps identify what type a file is at a glance.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: File name extensions are hidden
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows the file extension (e.g. .txt, .exe) after every filename in Explorer. Helps identify what type a file is at a glance.

Entries.Name: File Transfer Dialog
Entries.Detail: Controls whether the file copy and move dialog shows full details like speed and time remaining by default.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: File transfer dialog shown in compact mode
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether the file copy and move dialog shows full details like speed and time remaining by default.

Entries.Name: First Logon Animation
Entries.Detail: Shows a brief welcome animation and the Hi screen the first time you sign in after a major Windows update.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: First sign-in animation after upgrade is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows a brief welcome animation and the Hi screen the first time you sign in after a major Windows update.

Entries.Name: Folder Merge Conflicts
Entries.Detail: Shows a warning when merging folders that contain files with the same name. Disable to silently overwrite files without a prompt.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Folder merge conflicts are hidden
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows a warning when merging folders that contain files with the same name. Disable to silently overwrite files without a prompt.

Entries.Name: Folder Separate Process
Entries.Description: Launch folder windows in separate process
Entries.Detail: Opens each Explorer folder window in its own process. If one crashes, the others stay open. Uses slightly more memory.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Folder windows share the same process
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Opens each Explorer folder window in its own process. If one crashes, the others stay open. Uses slightly more memory.

Entries.Name: Hidden Files and Folders
Entries.Description: Show hidden files, folders, and drives
Entries.Detail: Shows files and folders that are marked as hidden in File Explorer. Hidden items appear slightly transparent when visible.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Hidden files and folders are not shown
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows files and folders that are marked as hidden in File Explorer. Hidden items appear slightly transparent when visible.

Entries.Name: Item Check Boxes
Entries.Detail: Shows small checkboxes next to files and folders in Explorer to make it easier to select multiple items without holding Ctrl.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Item check boxes are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows small checkboxes next to files and folders in Explorer to make it easier to select multiple items without holding Ctrl.

Entries.Name: Item Selection Checkboxes
Entries.Detail: Adds checkboxes next to files and folders in Explorer for easier multi-selection without needing to hold Ctrl.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Item selection checkboxes are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Adds checkboxes next to files and folders in Explorer for easier multi-selection without needing to hold Ctrl.

Entries.Name: JPEG Wallpaper Quality
Entries.Detail: Windows compresses JPEG wallpapers when setting them as the desktop background. Setting to Max preserves full image quality.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: JPEG wallpaper quality set to Default
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Windows compresses JPEG wallpapers when setting them as the desktop background. Setting to Max preserves full image quality.

Entries.Name: Nav Pane All Folders
Entries.Description: All folders in Explorer navigation pane
Entries.Detail: Shows all local folders including hidden system folders in the Explorer left navigation pane.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: All folders not shown in navigation pane
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows all local folders including hidden system folders in the Explorer left navigation pane.

Entries.Name: Nav Pane Libraries
Entries.Detail: Shows the Libraries section (Documents, Music, Pictures, Videos) in the Explorer left navigation pane.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Libraries not shown in navigation pane
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows the Libraries section (Documents, Music, Pictures, Videos) in the Explorer left navigation pane.

Entries.Name: Navigation Pane Auto Expand
Entries.Description: Expand to open folder on navigation pane
Entries.Detail: The Explorer navigation pane automatically expands and scrolls to highlight the folder you are currently viewing.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Navigation pane does not expand to current folder
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The Explorer navigation pane automatically expands and scrolls to highlight the folder you are currently viewing.

Entries.Name: OneDrive File Explorer Ad
Entries.Description: Sync provider notification in File Explorer
Entries.Detail: OneDrive shows a promotional notification inside File Explorer encouraging you to sync files. Hiding removes this banner.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: OneDrive sync notification shown in Explorer
Entries.WhyThisMatters: OneDrive shows a promotional notification inside File Explorer encouraging you to sync files. Hiding removes this banner.

Entries.Name: Open File Explorer To
Entries.Detail: Sets the default location File Explorer opens to when launched from the taskbar or Start Menu.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: File Explorer opens to Quick Access
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets the default location File Explorer opens to when launched from the taskbar or Start Menu.

Entries.Name: Per-App Input Method
Entries.Description: Different input method per app window
Entries.Detail: Allows Windows to remember and switch to a different input language or keyboard layout per application window.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Same input method used across all app windows
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Allows Windows to remember and switch to a different input language or keyboard layout per application window.

Entries.Name: Protected OS Files
Entries.Detail: Shows protected Windows system files in File Explorer. These are hidden by default to prevent accidental modification or deletion.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Protected OS files are not shown
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows protected Windows system files in File Explorer. These are hidden by default to prevent accidental modification or deletion.

Entries.Name: PrtScn Opens Snipping Tool
Entries.Detail: Makes the Print Screen key open the Snipping Tool for interactive screen capture instead of silently copying to the clipboard.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Print Screen does not open Snipping Tool
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Makes the Print Screen key open the Snipping Tool for interactive screen capture instead of silently copying to the clipboard.

Entries.Name: Quick Access Frequent Folders
Entries.Description: Frequently used folders in Quick access
Entries.Detail: The Quick Access section in Explorer can show frequently visited folders. Hide to keep Quick Access showing only your pinned folders.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Frequently used folders shown in Quick Access
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The Quick Access section in Explorer can show frequently visited folders. Hide to keep Quick Access showing only your pinned folders.

Entries.Name: Quick Access Recent Files
Entries.Description: Recently used files in Quick access
Entries.Detail: The Quick Access section in Explorer can show recently opened files. Hide to keep Quick Access showing only your pinned folders.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Recently used files shown in Quick Access
Entries.WhyThisMatters: The Quick Access section in Explorer can show recently opened files. Hide to keep Quick Access showing only your pinned folders.

Entries.Name: Recent Shortcuts in Explorer
Entries.Detail: Shows recently and frequently accessed files and folders as shortcuts in the Explorer Quick Access section.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Recent/frequent item shortcuts in Explorer are shown
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows recently and frequently accessed files and folders as shortcuts in the Explorer Quick Access section.

Entries.Name: Recycle Bin Delete Confirmation
Entries.Detail: Shows a confirmation prompt before moving files to the Recycle Bin. Enable to prevent accidental deletion.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: No confirmation shown when deleting files
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows a confirmation prompt before moving files to the Recycle Bin. Enable to prevent accidental deletion.

Entries.Name: Restore Folder Windows at Logon
Entries.Description: Restore previous folder windows at logon
Entries.Detail: Reopens any Explorer folder windows that were open when you last logged off. Restores your working context on each login.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Previous folder windows not restored at logon
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Reopens any Explorer folder windows that were open when you last logged off. Restores your working context on each login.

Entries.Name: Share Context Menu
Entries.Detail: Adds a Share option to the right-click context menu for files, letting you share them via apps or Windows nearby sharing.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Share context menu item is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Adds a Share option to the right-click context menu for files, letting you share them via apps or Windows nearby sharing.

Entries.Name: Sharing Wizard
Entries.Detail: Enables the simplified file sharing wizard when you right-click a file or folder and choose to share it.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Sharing Wizard is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Enables the simplified file sharing wizard when you right-click a file or folder and choose to share it.

Entries.Name: Shortcut Arrow Icon
Entries.Detail: Shows or hides the small arrow overlay icon on shortcut files to indicate they are shortcuts and not the original files.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Shortcut arrow overlay is shown
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows or hides the small arrow overlay icon on shortcut files to indicate they are shortcuts and not the original files.

Entries.Name: Shortcut Suffix
Entries.Detail: Controls whether Windows appends - Shortcut to the name of new shortcuts created on the desktop.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: - Shortcut suffix added to new shortcuts
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Controls whether Windows appends - Shortcut to the name of new shortcuts created on the desktop.

Entries.Name: Snap Assist
Entries.Description: Show what to snap next to a window
Entries.Detail: When you snap a window to one side, Windows suggests other open windows to fill the remaining space. Disable to snap without suggestions.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Snap Assist is enabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: When you snap a window to one side, Windows suggests other open windows to fill the remaining space. Disable to snap without suggestions.

Entries.Name: Sync Provider Notifications
Entries.Detail: OneDrive and other sync providers can show promotional notifications inside Explorer. Disable to hide these banners.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Sync provider notifications in Explorer are disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: OneDrive and other sync providers can show promotional notifications inside Explorer. Disable to hide these banners.

Entries.Name: This PC on Desktop
Entries.Detail: Shows or hides the This PC icon on your desktop for quick access to drives and devices.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: This PC icon hidden on Desktop
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows or hides the This PC icon on your desktop for quick access to drives and devices.

Entries.Name: Thumbnail Cache
Entries.Detail: Windows saves thumbnail previews in a hidden database file to speed up folder browsing. Disabling prevents this cache from being stored on disk.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Thumbnail cache creation is disabled
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Windows saves thumbnail previews in a hidden database file to speed up folder browsing. Disabling prevents this cache from being stored on disk.

Entries.Name: Thumbnails
Entries.Detail: Shows image and video previews as thumbnails in Explorer instead of generic file type icons. Disabling shows icons only.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: File extension icons shown instead of thumbnails
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Shows image and video previews as thumbnails in Explorer instead of generic file type icons. Disabling shows icons only.

Entries.Name: Thumbs.db on Network
Entries.Detail: Windows creates hidden Thumbs.db files on network shares to cache thumbnails. Disabling prevents these files from being created on network locations.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Thumbs.db cache not created on network folders
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Windows creates hidden Thumbs.db files on network shares to cache thumbnails. Disabling prevents these files from being created on network locations.

Entries.Name: Windows Color Mode
Entries.Detail: Sets whether the Windows shell (taskbar, Start Menu, action center) uses Dark or Light mode.
Entries.WinDefaultDesc: Windows mode set to Light
Entries.WhyThisMatters: Sets whether the Windows shell (taskbar, Start Menu, action center) uses Dark or Light mode.
